## UNIT: Reassess Roadmap — Milestone M001 after S01

All relevant context has been preloaded below — the current roadmap, completed slice summary, project state, and decisions are inlined. Start working immediately without re-reading these files.

## Inlined Context (preloaded — do not re-read these files)

### Current Roadmap
Source: `.gsd/milestones/M001/M001-ROADMAP.md`

# M001: Launch-Ready Oriflect Website

**Vision:** Convert completed HTML mockups into a navigable, mobile-responsive React + Vite site deployable to Vercel and Lovable, with ROI calculator and client portal concept added.

## Success Criteria

- All 7 pages render with correct Axiom Navy design system
- React Router navigation works with no full-page reloads
- Active nav state highlights current page
- Mobile hamburger menu opens/closes correctly
- Contact form shows success state on submit
- ROI calculator computes and displays results
- `npm run build` produces clean dist/ with no errors

## Key Risks / Unknowns

- Tailwind token extraction from CDN config — hyphens in color names must survive
- lh3.googleusercontent.com image URLs may 403 in some environments

## Proof Strategy

- Token extraction risk → retire in S01 by running `npm run dev` and visually confirming colors render
- Image URLs → use as-is; note in UAT for user to replace with real assets

## Verification Classes

- Contract verification: `npm run build` passes, all routes render without 404
- Integration verification: React Router `<Link>` navigation tested across all pages
- UAT / human verification: visual design check against mockup screenshots

## Milestone Definition of Done

- All 7 pages navigable via React Router
- Shared Nav + Footer on every page
- Active nav state correct per page
- Mobile responsive hamburger works
- Contact form and newsletter show feedback states
- ROI calculator functional
- `npm run build` clean
- vercel.json present for SPA routing

## Requirement Coverage

- Covers: R001, R002, R003, R004, R005, R006, R007, R008, R009, R010, R011
- Out of scope: R012, R013, R014

## Slices

- [x] **S01: Vite + React + Tailwind Scaffold** `risk:high` `depends:[]`
  > After this: `npm run dev` serves a blank Oriflect shell at localhost:5173 with all design tokens loaded and fonts rendering

- [ ] **S02: Shared Nav + Footer Components** `risk:medium` `depends:[S01]`
  > After this: Nav and Footer render on a test page; nav links highlight active state; mobile hamburger opens/closes

- [ ] **S03: Home + Services Pages** `risk:medium` `depends:[S02]`
  > After this: Home and Services pages fully rendered from mockup content with working nav

- [ ] **S04: About + Blog + Contact Pages** `risk:medium` `depends:[S02]`
  > After this: All 5 core pages navigable; contact form and newsletter show success feedback

- [ ] **S05: Mobile Polish + Design System Pass** `risk:low` `depends:[S03,S04]`
  > After this: Site is fully responsive at all breakpoints; design system consistent with DESIGN.md spec

- [ ] **S06: ROI Calculator + Client Portal Page** `risk:medium` `depends:[S04]`
  > After this: /roi page has working calculator with real computation; /portal renders concept dashboard

## Boundary Map

### S01 → S02
Produces:
- Vite + React project at project root
- `tailwind.config.js` with full Axiom Navy token set
- `src/main.jsx` entry point
- `src/App.jsx` with React Router setup (placeholder routes)
- `src/index.css` with base styles + font imports

Consumes:
- nothing (first slice)

### S01 → S03, S04, S05, S06
Produces:
- All of the above

### S02 → S03, S04, S05, S06
Produces:
- `src/components/Nav.jsx` — sticky nav with active state via useLocation
- `src/components/Footer.jsx` — shared footer
- `src/components/Layout.jsx` — wraps Nav + children + Footer

Consumes from S01:
- Tailwind config tokens
- React Router context

### S03 → S05
Produces:
- `src/pages/Home.jsx`
- `src/pages/Services.jsx`

### S04 → S05, S06
Produces:
- `src/pages/About.jsx`
- `src/pages/Blog.jsx`
- `src/pages/Contact.jsx` with form state
- Newsletter subscribe state in Blog.jsx

### S05 → S06
Produces:
- Responsive polish applied to all pages
- No new interfaces, just quality

### S06 (terminal)
Produces:
- `src/pages/ROICalculator.jsx` — stateful calculator
- `src/pages/ClientPortal.jsx` — concept dashboard
- Routes /roi and /portal registered in App.jsx

### Project
Source: `.gsd/PROJECT.md`

# Oriflect Website

## What This Is

Multi-page React + Vite website for Oriflect, an AI consulting firm targeting SMBs. The site establishes Oriflect as a premium "Intelligence Architect" brand using the Axiom Navy design system (deep navy + muted gold, 0px radius, editorial layout). Five core pages plus ROI calculator and client portal concept.

## Core Value

A deployable, visually distinctive site that converts visitors into discovery call bookings.

## Current State

Design mockups complete for all 5 pages (Home, Services, About, Blog, Contact) as standalone HTML files in `stitch_oriflect_landing_page/`. Design system documented in `stitch_oriflect_landing_page/axiom_navy/DESIGN.md`. Now assembling into a React + Vite project.

## Architecture / Key Patterns

- React + Vite + Tailwind CSS (CDN config extracted to tailwind.config.js)
- React Router for client-side navigation
- Shared Layout wrapper (Nav + Footer)
- Pages in `src/pages/`, components in `src/components/`
- Static HTML output — no backend

## Capability Contract

See `.gsd/REQUIREMENTS.md`

## Milestone Sequence

- [ ] M001: Launch-Ready Site — All pages built, navigable, mobile-responsive, deployable to Vercel/Lovable

---

### Requirements
Source: `.gsd/REQUIREMENTS.md`

# Requirements

## Active

### R001 — 5-page site with working React Router navigation
- Class: primary-user-loop
- Status: active
- Description: Home, Services, About, Blog, Contact all navigable via React Router with no full-page reloads
- Why it matters: Core product — without navigation the site doesn't function
- Source: user
- Primary owning slice: M001/S03
- Supporting slices: M001/S04
- Validation: unmapped

### R002 — Shared Nav with active-state highlighting
- Class: primary-user-loop
- Status: active
- Description: Navigation component shows active page via gold underline/color; present on every page
- Why it matters: Orientation and brand consistency
- Source: user
- Primary owning slice: M001/S02
- Supporting slices: none
- Validation: unmapped

### R003 — Shared Footer consistent across all pages
- Class: primary-user-loop
- Status: active
- Description: Footer with ORIFLECT logo, nav links, copyright renders on every page
- Why it matters: Brand closure on every page
- Source: user
- Primary owning slice: M001/S02
- Supporting slices: none
- Validation: unmapped

### R004 — Axiom Navy design system applied faithfully
- Class: quality-attribute
- Status: active
- Description: 0px border radius throughout, surface hierarchy (surface/surface-container-low/etc.), muted gold primary, ghost borders, dot-matrix grid backgrounds, Space Grotesk + Inter fonts
- Why it matters: Differentiating brand — if the design regresses to generic it undermines the product
- Source: user
- Primary owning slice: M001/S01
- Supporting slices: M001/S02, M001/S03, M001/S04, M001/S05
- Validation: unmapped

### R005 — Mobile-responsive hamburger nav
- Class: launchability
- Status: active
- Description: Navigation collapses to hamburger on mobile; menu opens/closes; all links work
- Why it matters: Non-negotiable for launch — majority of visits will be mobile
- Source: inferred
- Primary owning slice: M001/S05
- Supporting slices: none
- Validation: unmapped

### R006 — Contact form with visible feedback state
- Class: primary-user-loop
- Status: active
- Description: Contact form shows success message on submit (client-side); required field validation
- Why it matters: Primary conversion action — broken form loses leads
- Source: inferred
- Primary owning slice: M001/S04
- Supporting slices: none
- Validation: unmapped

### R007 — Blog newsletter subscribe with feedback state
- Class: primary-user-loop
- Status: active
- Description: Newsletter email input shows success/error feedback on submit
- Why it matters: Secondary conversion action for the blog
- Source: inferred
- Primary owning slice: M001/S04
- Supporting slices: none
- Validation: unmapped

### R008 — Deployable to Vercel + importable to Lovable
- Class: launchability
- Status: active
- Description: React + Vite + Tailwind project structure; npm run dev starts; npm run build produces dist/; standard enough for Lovable import
- Why it matters: User's explicit launch target
- Source: user
- Primary owning slice: M001/S01
- Supporting slices: none
- Validation: unmapped

### R009 — About page founder story content
- Class: core-capability
- Status: active
- Description: About page renders Edward Griggs founder story, mission statement, core values — Phase 3 content refinement
- Why it matters: Trust-building page — central to lead conversion
- Source: user
- Primary owning slice: M001/S04
- Supporting slices: none
- Validation: unmapped

### R010 — Interactive ROI Calculator
- Class: differentiator
- Status: active
- Description: /roi page with inputs (employees, hours saved, hourly rate) that compute projected annual savings and payback period
- Why it matters: Converts skeptical SMB owners by making the value proposition concrete and personal
- Source: user
- Primary owning slice: M001/S06
- Supporting slices: none
- Validation: unmapped

### R011 — Client Portal concept page
- Class: differentiator
- Status: active
- Description: /portal page showing a concept dashboard UI — project status, deliverables, communication log
- Why it matters: Signals professionalism and positions Oriflect as a long-term partner
- Source: user
- Primary owning slice: M001/S06
- Supporting slices: none

## Out of Scope

### Decisions
Source: `.gsd/DECISIONS.md`

# Decisions Register

<!-- Append-only. Never edit or remove existing rows. -->

| # | When | Scope | Decision | Choice | Rationale | Revisable? |
|---|------|-------|----------|--------|-----------|------------|
| D001 | M001 | arch | Frontend framework | React + Vite | Lovable runs React natively; Vercel deploys Vite out of the box; no build complexity | No |
| D002 | M001 | library | CSS framework | Tailwind CSS via npm | Extracted from CDN config in mockups; needed for tree-shaking in production build | No |
| D003 | M001 | library | Routing | React Router v6 | Standard for React SPAs; Vercel SPA routing via vercel.json | No |
| D004 | M001 | convention | No TypeScript | Plain JS (.jsx) | Simpler for Lovable import and visual editing; user did not request TS | Yes — if Lovable supports TS |
| D005 | M001 | arch | Form handling | Client-side only with simulated success state | No backend in scope; forms show feedback without submission | Yes — when backend added |
| D006 | M001 | convention | Images | Keep lh3.googleusercontent.com URLs from mockups | Fastest path to launch; user can replace with real assets later | Yes — production asset replacement |
| D007 | S01 | verification | S01 verification strategy | Build + grep assertions (no test framework) | S01 is a scaffold slice — contract proof via `npm run build` + token grep is sufficient; no UI logic to unit test | Yes — introduce Playwright in S02 or S05 if visual regression needed |
| D008 | S01 | convention | Project subdirectory | Vite project at `oriflect/` not workspace root | Scaffold was created pre-GSD; deviation is acceptable; all S02+ work targets `oriflect/src/` | No |

If a `GSD Skill Preferences` block is present in system context, use it to decide which skills to load and follow during reassessment, without relaxing required verification or artifact rules.

Then assess whether the remaining roadmap still makes sense given what was just built.

**Bias strongly toward "roadmap is fine."** Most of the time, the plan is still good. Only rewrite if you have concrete evidence that remaining slices need to change. Don't rewrite for cosmetic reasons, minor optimization, or theoretical improvements.

Ask yourself:
- Did this slice retire the risk it was supposed to? If not, does a remaining slice need to address it?
- Did new risks or unknowns emerge that should change slice ordering?
- Are the boundary contracts in the boundary map still accurate given what was actually built?
- Should any remaining slices be reordered, merged, split, or adjusted based on concrete evidence?
- Did assumptions in remaining slice descriptions turn out wrong?
- If `.gsd/REQUIREMENTS.md` exists: did this slice validate, invalidate, defer, block, or newly surface requirements?
- If `.gsd/REQUIREMENTS.md` exists: does the remaining roadmap still provide credible coverage for Active requirements, including launchability, primary user loop, continuity, and failure visibility where relevant?

### Success-Criterion Coverage Check

Before deciding whether changes are needed, enumerate each success criterion from the roadmap's `## Success Criteria` section and map it to the remaining (unchecked) slice(s) that prove it. Each criterion must have at least one remaining owning slice. If any criterion has no remaining owner after the proposed changes, flag it as a **blocking issue** — do not accept changes that leave a criterion unproved.

Format each criterion as a single line:

- `Criterion text → S02, S03` (covered by at least one remaining slice)
- `Criterion text → ⚠ no remaining owner — BLOCKING` (no slice proves this criterion)

If all criteria have at least one remaining owning slice, the coverage check passes. If any criterion has no remaining owner, resolve it before finalizing the assessment — either by keeping a slice that was going to be removed, adding coverage to another slice, or explaining why the criterion is no longer relevant.

**If the roadmap is still good:**

Write `C:\Users\edwar\Desktop\Oriflect Site\.gsd\milestones\M001\slices\S01\S01-ASSESSMENT.md` with a brief confirmation that roadmap coverage still holds after S01. If requirements exist, explicitly note whether requirement coverage remains sound.

**If changes are needed:**

1. Rewrite the remaining (unchecked) slices in `.gsd/milestones/M001/M001-ROADMAP.md`. Keep completed slices exactly as they are (`[x]`). Update the boundary map for changed slices. Update the proof strategy if risks changed. Update requirement coverage if ownership or scope changed.
2. Write `C:\Users\edwar\Desktop\Oriflect Site\.gsd\milestones\M001\slices\S01\S01-ASSESSMENT.md` explaining what changed and why — keep it brief and concrete.
3. If `.gsd/REQUIREMENTS.md` exists and requirement ownership or status changed, update it.
4. Commit: `docs(M001): reassess roadmap after S01`

**You MUST write the file `C:\Users\edwar\Desktop\Oriflect Site\.gsd\milestones\M001\slices\S01\S01-ASSESSMENT.md` before finishing.**

When done, say: "Roadmap reassessed."

arguments.content:
# S01 Roadmap Assessment

**Verdict:** Roadmap unchanged — no adjustments needed.

## What S01 Delivered

- Vite + React project at `oriflect/` (D008 deviation: subdirectory, not workspace root — confirmed acceptable)
- Full Axiom Navy token set extracted to `tailwind.config.js`
- React Router v6 wired in `App.jsx` with placeholder routes
- `src/pages/` and `src/components/` directories scaffolded
- `npm run build` passes clean

## Risk Retirement

S01's primary risk was Tailwind token extraction from CDN config (hyphenated color names). Verified: tokens present in `tailwind.config.js` with correct naming (e.g., `surface-container-low`). Risk retired.

Image URL risk (lh3.googleusercontent.com 403s) was noted as a known deviation — still stands, logged in D006, acceptable.

## Boundary Contracts

S01 → S02 contract is intact: `tailwind.config.js`, `src/main.jsx`, `src/App.jsx`, `src/index.css` all exist. S02 can consume immediately.

## Success Criterion Coverage

- All 7 pages render with correct Axiom Navy design system → S02, S03, S04, S05
- React Router navigation works with no full-page reloads → S02, S03
- Active nav state highlights current page → S02
- Mobile hamburger menu opens/closes correctly → S02, S05
- Contact form shows success state on submit → S04
- ROI calculator computes and displays results → S06
- `npm run build` produces clean dist/ with no errors → ✓ retired by S01

All criteria have at least one remaining owner. Coverage check passes.

## Requirement Coverage

No change to requirement ownership. R004 (Axiom Navy design system) and R008 (deployable structure) are now partially validated by S01. All other requirements remain on their assigned slices.

## No Changes Made

Roadmap slices S02–S06 are unchanged. Boundary map is accurate. Slice ordering is correct.
